items.prompt: What is the first step in data distribution modeling?
choices: Deploy the generator to production without validation
Analyze the real dataset to determine its underlying distribution
items.explain: Section 10.3.1 states that modeling begins by analyzing real data to identify the underlying distribution.

items.prompt: Monte Carlo simulation is especially useful when the underlying data distribution is complex or unknown.
items.explain: Section 10.3.1 describes Monte Carlo methods for unknown or complex distributions via repeated simulation.

items.prompt: Example 10.6 demonstrates sampling from which distribution?
choices: Uniform on zero to one only
Normal with mean 50 and standard deviation 10
items.explain: Example 10.6 specifies a normal distribution with mean 50 and standard deviation 10.

items.prompt: What domain does Example 10.7 use Monte Carlo simulation for?
items.rubric: Mention financial modeling, asset returns, or market/risk factors.
items.sample_answer: Financial modeling—generating synthetic asset returns under varied risk factors and market conditions.

items.prompt: Statistical synthetic data methods can support privacy by sharing simulated distributions instead of individual records.
items.explain: Section 10.3 notes statistical methods can preserve privacy while generating useful training and testing data.
